title: Update documentation for workspace initialization and local error-log artifacts
summary: Update release-facing documentation so the v0.4.6 startup contract clearly requires one-time `workspace_initialize(cwd)` binding before canonical state restore, and explain that failed tool executions are persisted in `.agent/errors.jsonl` for local debugging and post-session inspection.
acceptance_criteria: Documentation explains that `workspace_initialize(cwd)` is the mandatory first startup step before reading `tx_state`, `tx_event_log`, or `handoff`.
Documentation explains the unresolved-startup behavior for Zed multi-project launches where process CWD may be '/'.
Documentation explains that failed tool executions are appended to `.agent/errors.jsonl` with tool context for local troubleshooting.
Documentation remains aligned with the updated `.rules` contract and the scaffolded `.rules` generated by `zed-agentops-init.sh`.

steps.description: Review the updated startup contract in the plan, `.rules`, and scaffold script so the documentation matches runtime behavior.
Update README documentation to describe one-time workspace initialization, unresolved startup under '/', and expected behavior for normal single-project launches.
Document the new `.agent/errors.jsonl` artifact, including what kinds of tool failures are recorded and why it complements the Zed agent panel.
Cross-check English and Japanese documentation for consistency with the release plan and acceptance criteria.

notes: Keep the documentation focused on operator-facing behavior, not internal implementation details unless they are needed to explain the startup contract.
Make sure the explanation distinguishes between transient panel-only errors and durable local error logs under `.agent/errors.jsonl`.
